title: Problem-Solving Puppets
description: Cast two puppets, watch them hit a real kid-sized problem and try an idea — then judge it: happy face or sad face?
learningGoals: Watch choices and their consequences acted out, then judge them — show, don't tell
Practice sharing, turn-taking, and asking for help with modeled kind words
Learn that unkind choices make friends sad — and that you can always try again
interactionModel: puppet-theater: live bone-rig puppets act the problem, then ONE randomly-drawn choice plays out; the show pauses and the child judges it with a red unhappy-face or green happy-face button
feedbackModel: Correct judgments sparkle and are confirmed by the narrator; after judging an unkind choice the puppets act a kind repair so every scene ends warm. Wrong judgments get a gentle nudge and a re-ask. One big celebration ends the show. No fail states.
credits: First shipping game on the shared bone-rig puppet engine. Puppet characters, acting clips, and voices from shared/characters/. Built with Claude for QLOBE Kids.
